title: Naming the Plants: A Corpus-Attested Sanskrit-to-Linnaean Crosswalk from the Monier-Williams Dictionary
description: The botanical layer of Monier-Williams' A Sanskrit-English Dictionary (1899) is extracted as the first machine-readable Sanskrit-to-Linnaean species crosswalk from a Cologne dictionary: 8,923 occurrences, 7,063 headwords, 1,223 species. The dataset includes lexicographer-only markings (sense attested only in indigenous synonym lexicons) and Digital Corpus of Sanskrit attestation with a homograph-controlled join for defensible corpus coverage statistics.
